emulator-gb.description: Game Boy (DMG / LR35902 CPU) emulator oracle. Verifies an implementation by diffing its per-instruction execution trace against a known-correct reference trace, then asserting the standard Blargg ROM serial-output pass markers.

assets.name: blargg-cpu-instrs
assets.purpose: Blargg's cpu_instrs test ROM suite — the de-facto correctness battery for CPU opcode/flag behavior. Each sub-ROM prints PASS/FAIL over the serial link.
assets.fetch: git submodule or curl per-rom; do NOT copy ROM bytes into this repo

assets.name: gameboy-doctor-truth-traces
assets.purpose: gameboy-doctor reference CPU state logs (one line per instruction: registers + PC + opcode bytes). The trace-diff compares the implementation's emitted log line-for-line against these.
assets.fetch: clone reference repo; truth logs live under its truth/ tree, keyed by test rom number

exchange-lob.description: Limit-order-book matching-engine oracle. Replays seeded order flow through both the implementation and an independent naive O(n^2) reference matcher, then asserts the resulting fills, book state, and event streams are identical across all seeds.

assets.name: reference-matcher
assets.purpose: Independently authored naive O(n^2) price-time-priority matching engine. Deliberately simple (correctness over speed) so it shares no code path with the optimized implementation under test.

assets.name: seeded-lob-replay
assets.purpose: Deterministic seeded order-flow generator + recorded replay fixtures. Each seed produces a reproducible stream of limit/market/cancel orders driving the differential comparison.

raytracer-calib.description: Ray-tracer rendering oracle used only to calibrate the gate harness (not as a pass/fail gate for shipped work). Renders a fixed scene and issues a verdict by comparing output against a reference image via SSIM at a high similarity threshold.

assets.name: reference-render
assets.purpose: Golden reference render of the calibration scene at fixed resolution/seed. The verdict gate scores the implementation's render against this image with SSIM.
